query: We just had a strategy offsite — now turn it into a committed plan.
Audit whether our existing plan can actually be steered.
Build a plan with tripwires, signposts, and a pullback ladder.
What's the first binding crux and max tolerable loss for this strategy?
How do we know if our assumptions are breaking? Set up trigger points.
Convert this strategic choice into funded, synchronized, contingent commitments.
The plan looks good on paper but I'm not sure it's governable.
Triage this bug report and drive it through the lifecycle.
Write a failing test first for the retry scheduler.
Draft our Q3 OKRs for the team.
Plan this sprint's tickets for the mobile team.
Generate a CAD model of a mounting bracket.
Do a security review of the credential-loading path.
